subject: DevOps
title: GitHub Actions
description: A quick, practical guide to GitHub Actions.

hook.text: GitHub Actions turned every GitHub repo into a CI/CD platform. No external tool needed. It just works.

buildup.text: Workflows live in YAML files inside your repo. Push code, and GitHub runs the workflow automatically.

discovery.text: The marketplace has thousands of reusable actions: deploy to AWS, post to Slack, run security scans.

twist.text: Matrix builds test across many versions at once. Test Node 18, 20, and 22 in parallel with one config.

climax.text: Free for public repos. For private repos, the free tier includes 2000 minutes per month. Plenty for small teams.

punchline.text: GitHub Actions: CI/CD built into where your code already lives.

quiz.question: Where are GitHub Actions workflow files stored?
options: In YAML files inside the repository itself
In a separate GitHub CI service
In a cloud dashboard outside of Git
